blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I've been trying to figure out what to say since I found out Thursday night. I wrote it in my head a hundred times.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Take your time.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Since the first day I noticed you.
I went still. Something tightened in my chest.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I know we barely — I know you probably think I'm doing a lot right now. But I'm already gone and I —

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I need you to know.
Her voice caught on the last word. Not dramatically. Just — it wasn't fully there for a second. She steadied.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: You were captivating to me from the beginning. Every time you looked at me I could feel it — like you were actually seeing me, not just looking. I used to find reasons to walk by. I used to ask Jonathan about you.
She asked Jonathan about me. The whole time. While I was on those steps trying to figure out how to say something.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I kept thinking — he'll say it today. He'll say it this time on the steps. And then you did, and then Wednesday happened, and then my parents sat me down Thursday night and —
She stopped. Longer this time. I could hear the street around her — a car going by somewhere, a distant sound she didn't recognize yet.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: My parents taught me love is serious. That it's something you invest in and you don't waste. And I wanted —

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I wanted you to be that person for me.
Something cracked open. That sentence. The way she said it — like she'd been holding it so carefully and it finally slipped out onto the steps of a house that wasn't home yet.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I'm scared you're going to find someone and I'm going to have to make peace with that from a thousand miles away. I've been sitting on these steps trying to make peace with it and I —

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Sorry. Give me —
She couldn't finish it. I heard her put the phone away from her face for a second. A breath. Two. Then she came back.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I'm sorry. I told myself I wasn't going to —

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Don't apologize.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I just wanted you to know it was real. Whatever you thought about me — it was real. I wasn't just passing time.

Her voice went again. I could feel it through the phone — the weight of everything she'd been carrying since Thursday night, coming through all at once. Sitting on the front steps of a strange house in Atlanta, alone for the first time all day, finally saying it.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: My heart melted every time you looked at me.
She said it quietly. Like a fact she'd never meant to say out loud.

blocks.prompt: She said everything. What does Larry say?

options.label: "I felt the same way. I just — I didn't know how to say it."
"Is there any way — is Atlanta really —"
"I don't know what to say right now."
[He doesn't say anything. He just stays on the line.]
